proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher levels of feature_057 (ordinal 0/1/2) are associated with a lower probability of objective_response.
Higher values of feature_011 (continuous, range 0-23.6) are associated with a lower probability of objective_response.
Higher values of feature_006 (continuous, range 20-100) are associated with a lower probability of objective_response.

analyses.code: from scipy.stats import spearmanr; spearmanr(df['feature_057'], df['objective_response'])
analyses.result_summary: Spearman rho=-0.0946 between feature_057 and objective_response (p=7.7e-100). Response rate falls monotonically across feature_057 levels: 0→21.3% (n=17,592), 1→15.5% (n=24,971), 2→11.2% (n=7,437).
Spearman rho=-0.0511 between feature_011 and objective_response (p=3.1e-30). Mean feature_011 in non-responders 3.95 vs responders 3.38.
Spearman rho=-0.0415 between feature_006 and objective_response (p=1.6e-20). Mean feature_006 in non-responders 60.3 vs responders 58.2.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_035=1 have a higher probability of objective_response than patients with feature_035=0.
Higher values of feature_092 (continuous, range 0.5-500) are associated with a lower probability of objective_response.
Higher values of feature_099 (continuous, range 1.7-5.5) are associated with a higher probability of objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Response rate 22.1% in feature_035=1 (n=3624) vs 16.5% in feature_035=0 (n=46,376). Risk difference +0.0563; chi-square p=3.5e-18.
Spearman rho=-0.0267 (p=2.3e-09). Quartile response rates monotonic from Q1 (18.0%) to Q4 (15.6%).
Spearman rho=+0.0216 (p=1.3e-06). Direction positive: higher feature_099 → higher response. Effect is small.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher values of feature_063 (continuous, range 0.03-300) are associated with a lower probability of objective_response.
Patients with feature_093=1 have a higher probability of objective_response than feature_093=0.
Response rates differ by feature_005 (race/ethnicity: white, hispanic, black, asian, other).

analyses.result_summary: Spearman rho=-0.0186, p=3.3e-05. Higher feature_063 modestly associated with lower response.
Response rate 18.5% in feature_093=1 (n=4025) vs 16.8% in feature_093=0 (n=45,975). Risk difference +0.0172, chi-square p=0.0056.
Response by race/ethnicity: white 17.3%, hispanic 16.7%, asian 16.3%, black 15.7%, other 14.7%. Chi-square p=0.0096; spread (max-min)=0.026.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patient age (feature_078, range 18-92, mean 68) is associated with objective_response.
Insurance type (feature_087: medicare/private/medicaid/uninsured) is associated with objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: No association: mean feature_078 in non-responders 67.98 vs responders 68.03. Spearman rho ≈0, no monotonic trend across age quartiles (16.7%, 17.4%, 16.6%, 16.9%). Hypothesis refuted.
Response rates: medicare 17.2%, medicaid 16.9%, private 16.7%, uninsured 16.6%. Spread <1 percentage point. Not significant. Hypothesis refuted.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher levels of the multi-level ordinal feature_125 (0-4) are associated with a lower probability of objective_response.
Higher levels of the multi-level ordinal feature_018 (0-10) are associated with a higher probability of objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Spearman rho=-0.0104, p=0.020. Weakly significant negative trend: response 17.4% at level 0 falling to 15.7% at level 4.
Logistic OR 1.004 per level (p=0.34). Response rates by level largely flat (16-19%) with slight uptick at level 10 (18.99%, n=758). Not significant overall; hypothesis refuted.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative association between feature_011 and objective_response is approximately monotonic across feature_011 quartiles.
The negative association between feature_006 and objective_response is approximately monotonic across feature_006 quartiles.

analyses.result_summary: Response rates by feature_011 quartile (3 unique bins because of ties at low values): Q1 18.6% (n=25,138), Q2 16.8% (n=12,562), Q3 13.6% (n=12,300). Monotonic decline; hypothesis supported.
Response rates by feature_006 quartile: Q1 18.96%, Q2 17.48%, Q3 16.50%, Q4 14.67%. Strictly monotonic decline of ~4.3 percentage points across the range; hypothesis supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treating feature_057 as three categorical levels rather than linear gives larger effects: each successive level (1 vs 0, 2 vs 0) reduces odds of response.

analyses.result_summary: OR vs feature_057=0: level 1 OR=0.679 (p=2e-52), level 2 OR=0.466 (p=5.3e-77). Levels are ordered as expected (more decrement at level 2). Linear ordinal coding captures most of the signal.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_057, feature_011, feature_006, feature_035, feature_092, feature_099, and feature_063 each retain a statistically independent effect on objective_response in a joint multivariable logistic model.

analyses.code: Logit(objective_response ~ feature_057 + feature_011 + feature_006 + feature_035 + feature_092 + feature_099 + feature_063 + feature_078)
analyses.result_summary: All seven candidates remain significant. Adjusted logit coefs: feature_057 -0.383 (OR 0.682, p=1.3e-96); feature_011 -0.040 (OR 0.961, p=1.3e-34); feature_006 -0.006 (OR 0.994, p=7.0e-20); feature_035 +0.360 (OR 1.43, p=1.9e-17); feature_092 -0.002 (OR 0.998, p=1.1e-05); feature_099 +0.118 (OR 1.13, p=9.3e-07); feature_063 -0.007 (OR 0.993, p=1.3e-06). Age (feature_078) again null (p=0.77).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The race-associated difference in response (feature_005) persists as a significant residual effect after adjustment for the seven leading clinical predictors and insurance.
Insurance type (feature_087) has no residual effect on response after adjustment for the seven leading clinical predictors and race.

analyses.code: LR test: full vs model with race dummies removed (after top-7 + insurance)
analyses.result_summary: LR statistic=13.49 on 4 df, p=0.0091. Race effect remains significant after adjustment, with the white group highest (logit coef +0.079 vs reference asian) and 'other' lowest (-0.117). Magnitude small (max-min ~2.6 percentage points). Hypothesis supported.

analyses.code: LR test: full vs model with insurance dummies removed
analyses.result_summary: LR statistic=2.51 on 3 df, p=0.47. Insurance is not a significant residual predictor. Hypothesis supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a multiplicative (logit-scale) interaction between feature_057 and feature_035 such that the benefit of feature_035=1 differs across feature_057 levels.

analyses.code: Logit(... + feature_057*feature_035) vs main-effects model; LR test
analyses.result_summary: Interaction coefficient +0.031 on logit scale; LR p=0.633, Wald p=0.633. No multiplicative interaction. The feature_035 benefit is similar across feature_057 strata: risk difference +0.061 at level 0, +0.053 at level 1, +0.044 at level 2 (each significant, descending with baseline rate). Hypothesis refuted.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a logit-scale interaction between feature_057 and feature_011: the strength of the negative feature_011 effect varies with feature_057 level.

analyses.code: Logit(... + feature_057*feature_011); also stratified logits within each feature_057 level
analyses.result_summary: Interaction coef -0.0025; LR p=0.62. Stratified logistic slopes for feature_011: feature_057=0 → -0.041 (p=5e-16); =1 → -0.037 (p=1e-14); =2 → -0.052 (p=5e-07). Slopes are similar; hypothesis refuted.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a logit-scale interaction between feature_011 and feature_006 (their joint negative effect is more than additive).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coef +0.00027; LR p=0.111. Borderline trend: positive sign would imply a slight attenuation of joint negative effect, but the interaction does not reach significance. Hypothesis not supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a logit-scale interaction between feature_099 and feature_035: the benefit of feature_035=1 is larger when feature_099 is lower.

analyses.code: Logit(... + feature_099*feature_035); LR test; quartile-stratified risk differences
analyses.result_summary: Interaction coef -0.186 on logit scale (negative → the feature_035 benefit shrinks as feature_099 rises). LR p=0.025, Wald p=0.025. Risk difference for feature_035 by feature_099 quartile: Q1 +0.069, Q2 +0.053, Q3 +0.067, Q4 +0.034. The pattern is generally consistent with a smaller benefit in the highest-feature_099 quartile.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a logit-scale interaction between feature_063 and feature_011 (both already main negative predictors).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coef -0.00077; LR p=0.076. Borderline negative interaction (joint effect slightly more than additive on logit scale) but not significant at the 0.05 level. Hypothesis not supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a logit-scale interaction between feature_006 and feature_035 such that the feature_035 benefit varies with feature_006.

analyses.code: Logit(... + feature_006*feature_035); LR test; quartile-stratified risk differences
analyses.result_summary: Logit interaction coef +0.0023; LR p=0.30. Stratified risk differences for feature_035 across feature_006 quartiles: Q1 +0.036, Q2 +0.095, Q3 +0.038, Q4 +0.056. No monotonic interaction pattern and the formal test is not significant. Hypothesis refuted.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjustment for the seven leading clinical predictors, only a small subset of the remaining 80 binary features show significant residual association with objective_response (i.e., most binary features are noise).

analyses.code: For each binary feature c: Logit(objective_response ~ top6 + c); collect p-values
analyses.result_summary: After adjusting for feature_057, feature_011, feature_006, feature_092, feature_099, feature_063 (and dropping feature_035 from the adjustment set so its candidacy is tested), only 4/80 binary features have p<0.05 and only 1 (feature_035) has p<0.001. Top adjusted: feature_035 OR=1.43 (p=1.9e-17), feature_093 OR=1.13 (p=0.0036), feature_014 OR=1.06 (p=0.018), feature_121 OR=0.91 (p=0.040). Consistent with most binary features being noise.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_093 retains a significant positive association with objective_response after adjustment for feature_035 and the other top predictors (i.e., it is independent from feature_035).

analyses.code: Logit(... + feature_035 + feature_093) compared with model excluding feature_093
analyses.result_summary: feature_093 adjusted logit coef +0.125 (OR 1.13), p=0.0036, after including feature_035 as a covariate. Effect is independent of feature_035 and consistent in direction with the univariate finding. Hypothesis supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_121=1 have a lower probability of objective_response after adjustment, opposite in sign to feature_035 and feature_093.

analyses.result_summary: Univariate: 15.6% in feature_121=1 (n=4143) vs 17.0% in feature_121=0 (chi-square p=0.025). Adjusted OR=0.91, p=0.040. Direction confirmed (negative); modest residual effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The risk-difference benefit of feature_035=1 is consistent in direction across all three feature_057 levels (i.e., feature_035 helps regardless of feature_057).

analyses.code: Stratified risk differences and chi-square within each feature_057 level
analyses.result_summary: Risk difference (feature_035=1 minus =0) within each level: feature_057=0 → +0.061 (chi2 p=1.7e-07), =1 → +0.053 (p=2.5e-09), =2 → +0.044 (p=2.6e-03). Direction consistent and statistically significant in every stratum. Hypothesis supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The race-associated effect on response (feature_005) reflects a relative deficit in the 'black' and 'other' categories vs 'white' rather than 'white' having a uniquely higher response.

analyses.code: Adjusted logit with race dummies (asian as reference)
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted logit coefs: white +0.079 (NS, p=0.13 vs asian), hispanic +0.043 (NS), black -0.030 (NS), other -0.117 (NS, p=0.25). The omnibus race test is significant (p=0.0091) but no individual contrast clears 0.05; the spread is concentrated in the white-vs-other contrast (~0.20 logit ≈ ~3 percentage points). Refined hypothesis supported in pattern but no single contrast reaches significance.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among patients with the highest disease-burden phenotype (feature_057=2), the negative effect of high feature_011 is larger in absolute logit terms than at lower feature_057 levels.

analyses.code: Stratified Logit(objective_response ~ feature_011) within each feature_057 level
analyses.result_summary: Slope of feature_011 (logit per unit): feature_057=0 → -0.041; =1 → -0.037; =2 → -0.052. Direction matches hypothesis (steeper at level 2) but the magnitudes are similar and the interaction term is not significant (p=0.62). Hypothesis weakly supported in point estimates only.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_092 (highly skewed, range 0.5-500) has a roughly log-linear association with response: log(feature_092) gives an equivalent or stronger association than feature_092 in raw scale.

analyses.result_summary: Spearman rho is identical for log(feature_092) and feature_092 (-0.027, p=2.3e-09) because Spearman is rank-based. Effect size is small and the log transform does not change the conclusion: higher feature_092 → lower response.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A multivariable model using only the seven main predictors (feature_057, feature_011, feature_006, feature_035, feature_092, feature_099, feature_063) provides modest discrimination of objective_response (AUC > 0.55).

analyses.code: Logit(top7); compute AUC via Mann-Whitney U
analyses.result_summary: Full-model AUC = 0.597 (Mann-Whitney). Pseudo-R² ≈ 0.018 with overall LLR p=7.8e-174. Modest but real discrimination consistent with multiple small effects; hypothesis supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_125 (ordinal 0-4) shows a small but real negative ordinal trend with response.
Multi-int features feature_096, feature_064, feature_045, feature_042 do NOT show clear monotonic trends with response (i.e., they are not ordinal predictors here).

analyses.code: Spearman feature_125 vs response; group means
analyses.result_summary: Spearman rho=-0.0104, p=0.020. Response 17.4% at level 0 to 15.7% at level 4. Hypothesis weakly supported.

analyses.code: Spearman of each multi-int feature vs response; visual check of group means
analyses.result_summary: feature_096 (rho not significant; means 16.5-17.7% non-monotone), feature_064 (slight upward by level), feature_045 (small negative), feature_042 (small negative). None clean ordinal predictors. Hypothesis supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Synthesis: the dominant story in this cohort is (a) a strong stage/risk-like feature_057 gradient, (b) negative continuous predictors (feature_011, feature_006, feature_092, feature_063), (c) a positive binary feature_035 effect (~+5.6 percentage points unadjusted; OR 1.43 adjusted), (d) a positive feature_099 effect, (e) a small feature_099×feature_035 attenuation, and (f) small but significant residual race differences with no insurance effect and no age effect.

analyses.code: Comprehensive multivariable logistic with race + insurance + top-7
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted ORs (95% CI): feature_057 0.682 (0.66-0.71), feature_011 0.961 (0.95-0.97), feature_006 0.994 (0.99-1.00), feature_035 1.434 (1.32-1.56), feature_092 0.998 (0.997-0.999), feature_099 1.125 (1.07-1.18), feature_063 0.993 (0.99-1.00). Age and insurance not significant. Race omnibus p=0.009 with white vs other contrast largest. feature_099×feature_035 interaction p=0.025 (only significant pairwise interaction among the top-7). Synthesis supported.
